authors: Junde Wu, Minhao Hu, Jiayuan Zhu, Jiazhen Pan, Yuyuan Liu, Min Xu, Yueming Jin
title: Git Context Controller: Manage the Context of LLM-based Agents like Git
storage_format: Versioned .GCC/ directory: main.md (global roadmap, milestones, to-do list shared across branches), branches/{name}/commit.md (progress summaries: Branch Purpose + Previous Progress + This Commit's Contribution), log.md (fine-grained OTA traces), metadata.yaml (file structure, dependencies, configs). All plain-text, checkpointed via real git commits.
agent_framework: swe_agent (GCC integrated on top of SWE-Agent for SWE-Bench)
llm_used: GPT-5, GPT-4.1, DeepSeek-V3.1, GLM-4.5-Air, Qwen3-235B-A22B, Claude 4 Sonnet
benchmarks_used: BrowseComp-Plus (150 instances), SWE-Bench Verified (500 instances), SWE-Benchlite (300 instances)
performance_metrics: Pass@1, % Resolved, Tool Calls, % Correct Location (Line, Function, File)
baseline_compared: ReAct Agent, SWE Agent, Summary Agent, Folding Agent (controlled); 26 existing systems on SWE-Benchlite (CodeStory Aide, MarsCode, Honeycomb, MentatBot, etc.)
memory_size_over_time: Non-monotonic context length: grows with inference steps but periodically shrinks via summarization and retrieval; up to 100 branches/archives summarized (theoretical max 3.27M tokens)
main_result: SOTA on SWE-Bench Verified (Pass@1 0.802 with Claude 4 Sonnet, +13.6% vs baseline) and BrowseComp-Plus (0.834 with GPT-5). On SWE-Benchlite, it resolves 48.00% (144/300), surpassing 26 existing systems including commercial agents.
performance_vs_baseline: +6.2pp Pass@1 vs Folding Agent (best baseline) with Claude 4 Sonnet on SWE-Bench Verified (0.802 vs 0.740); +1.9pp BrowseComp-Plus with GPT-5 (0.834 vs 0.815)
cost_vs_baseline: $1.21/task vs $1.62 SWE-Agent, $2.51 Agentless on SWE-Benchlite (Claude 3.5 S); cost comparable to the best open-source systems despite much higher performance
limitations: Fixed 327K context window; retrieval window K=1 limits access to old commits; BRANCH and MERGE significantly increase tool calls; not tested under a fixed cost budget
validity_threats: Frontier proprietary models (GPT-5, Claude 4 Sonnet) may exhibit emergent capabilities that do not generalize; artificial benchmarks vs real production; reliance on large context windows (327K); ablation only with Claude 4 Sonnet
